description: Skills registry for skill discovery keywords, background operation rules, and advisory metadata. Validator routing is handled by validator-dispatcher.py.

sf-flow.reason: Flow tests execute in the org and can take several minutes

sf-testing.reason: Apex tests can run for extended periods depending on org size and test volume

sf-deploy.reason: Full deployments and org creation are long-running operations

sf-data.reason: Large data imports can take significant time

sf-datacloud-harmonize.reason: Identity resolution and data-graph refreshes can take several minutes to materialize results

sf-datacloud-segment.reason: Segment publishes and calculated insight runs often execute asynchronously and can take several minutes

sf-datacloud-retrieve.reason: Large Data Cloud query jobs are better handled asynchronously

sf-apex.description: Apex code development with validation and best practices
sf-apex.triggerWhen: user writes, reviews, or fixes Apex classes, triggers, test classes, batch/queueable/schedulable jobs, or touches .cls/.trigger files
sf-apex.doNotTriggerWhen: LWC JavaScript (use sf-lwc), Flow XML (use sf-flow), SOQL-only queries (use sf-soql), or non-Salesforce code

sf-flow.triggerWhen: user builds or edits record-triggered, screen, autolaunched, or scheduled flows, or touches .flow-meta.xml files
sf-flow.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex automation (use sf-apex), process builder migration questions only, or non-Flow declarative config (use sf-metadata)

sf-lwc.description: Lightning Web Components with SLDS compliance
sf-lwc.triggerWhen: user creates/edits LWC components, touches lwc/**/*.js, .html, .css, .js-meta.xml files, or asks about wire service, SLDS, or Jest LWC tests
sf-lwc.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex classes (use sf-apex), Aura components, or Visualforce

sf-ai-agentforce-persona.description: Deep persona design for Agentforce -- 12-attribute framework, brand input, archetype presets, Agent Builder & Agent Script encoding
sf-ai-agentforce-persona.triggerWhen: user designs agent personas, defines agent personality/identity, creates persona documents, encodes persona into Agent Builder fields or Agent Script, translates brand guidelines to agent voice, or asks about agent tone/voice/register
sf-ai-agentforce-persona.doNotTriggerWhen: building agent metadata (use sf-ai-agentforce), testing agents (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or Agent Script DSL (use sf-ai-agentscript)

sf-ai-agentforce.description: Standard Agentforce platform skill for Setup UI agent building, PromptTemplate, Models API, and GenAi metadata
sf-ai-agentforce.triggerWhen: user configures agents in Agent Builder, creates topics/actions, writes PromptTemplates, or touches .genAiFunction/.genAiPlugin/.promptTemplate metadata XML
sf-ai-agentforce.doNotTriggerWhen: Agent Script DSL .agent files (use sf-ai-agentscript), agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or persona design (use sf-ai-agentforce-persona)

sf-metadata.triggerWhen: user creates custom objects, fields, validation rules, or touches .object-meta.xml, .field-meta.xml, .profile-meta.xml files
sf-metadata.doNotTriggerWhen: permission set analysis (use sf-permissions), deploying metadata (use sf-deploy), or Flow XML (use sf-flow)

sf-deploy.triggerWhen: user deploys metadata, creates/manages scratch orgs or sandboxes, sets up CI/CD pipelines, or troubleshoots deployment errors with sf project deploy
sf-deploy.doNotTriggerWhen: writing Apex/LWC code (use sf-apex/sf-lwc), creating metadata XML (use sf-metadata), or querying org data (use sf-data)

sf-testing.triggerWhen: user runs Apex tests, checks code coverage, fixes failing tests, or touches *Test.cls / *_Test.cls files
sf-testing.doNotTriggerWhen: writing Apex production code (use sf-apex), Agentforce agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or Jest/LWC tests (use sf-lwc)

sf-soql.triggerWhen: user writes, optimizes, or debugs SOQL/SOSL queries, touches .soql files, or asks about relationship queries, aggregates, or query performance
sf-soql.doNotTriggerWhen: bulk data operations (use sf-data), Apex DML logic (use sf-apex), or report/dashboard queries

sf-data.triggerWhen: user creates test data, performs bulk import/export, uses sf data CLI commands, or needs data factory patterns for Apex tests
sf-data.doNotTriggerWhen: SOQL query writing only (use sf-soql), Apex test execution (use sf-testing), or metadata deployment (use sf-deploy)

sf-datacloud.description: Cross-phase Salesforce Data Cloud orchestration using the external community sf data360 runtime
sf-datacloud.triggerWhen: user needs a multi-phase Data Cloud workflow, manages data spaces or data kits, troubleshoots Data Cloud across phases, or explicitly references `sf data360` at a broad product level
sf-datacloud.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is isolated to a single Data Cloud phase, standard CRM SOQL, Apex-only work, or STDM/session tracing/parquet telemetry (use sf-ai-agentforce-observability)

sf-datacloud-connect.description: Data Cloud connections, connectors, and source discovery
sf-datacloud-connect.triggerWhen: user manages Data Cloud connections, connectors, source metadata, or tests a connection
sf-datacloud-connect.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is about data streams, DLOs, DMOs, SQL/search, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-prepare.description: Data Cloud ingestion, streams, DLOs, transforms, and Document AI
sf-datacloud-prepare.triggerWhen: user creates or manages Data Cloud streams, DLOs, transforms, or DocAI workflows
sf-datacloud-prepare.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is connector-only setup, harmonization, or pure query/search work

sf-datacloud-harmonize.description: Data Cloud DMOs, mappings, identity resolution, unified profiles, and data graphs
sf-datacloud-harmonize.triggerWhen: user works with Data Cloud DMOs, mappings, identity resolution, unified profiles, data graphs, or universal ID lookup
sf-datacloud-harmonize.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is only about streams/DLOs, segment design, SQL/search, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-segment.description: Data Cloud segments, calculated insights, and audience troubleshooting
sf-datacloud-segment.triggerWhen: user creates or publishes Data Cloud segments, manages calculated insights, or troubleshoots audience logic
sf-datacloud-segment.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is activation delivery, pure query/search work, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-act.description: Data Cloud activations, activation targets, and data actions
sf-datacloud-act.triggerWhen: user manages Data Cloud activations, activation targets, data actions, or downstream delivery setup
sf-datacloud-act.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is segment design, search/query work, or STDM/session tracing

sf-datacloud-retrieve.description: Data Cloud SQL, async query, search-index, vector-search, hybrid-search, and metadata introspection workflows
sf-datacloud-retrieve.triggerWhen: user runs Data Cloud SQL, describe, async queries, vector search, hybrid search, search-index workflows, or metadata introspection
sf-datacloud-retrieve.doNotTriggerWhen: the task is CRM SOQL-only, segment design, or STDM/session tracing/parquet analysis

sf-docs.description: Official Salesforce documentation retrieval guidance for hard-to-fetch online docs such as developer.salesforce.com and help.salesforce.com

sf-integration.triggerWhen: user sets up Named Credentials, External Services, REST/SOAP callouts, Platform Events, CDC, or touches .namedCredential-meta.xml files
sf-integration.doNotTriggerWhen: Connected App/OAuth config (use sf-connected-apps), Apex-only logic (use sf-apex), or data import/export (use sf-data)

sf-connected-apps.triggerWhen: user configures OAuth flows, JWT bearer auth, Connected Apps, or touches .connectedApp-meta.xml / .eca-meta.xml files
sf-connected-apps.doNotTriggerWhen: Named Credentials for callouts (use sf-integration), permission policies (use sf-permissions), or API endpoint code (use sf-apex)

sf-debug.triggerWhen: user analyzes debug logs, hits governor limits, reads stack traces, or touches .log files from Salesforce orgs
sf-debug.doNotTriggerWhen: running Apex tests (use sf-testing), fixing Apex code (use sf-apex), or Agentforce session tracing (use sf-ai-agentforce-observability)

sf-diagram-mermaid.triggerWhen: user says 'diagram', 'visualize', 'ERD', or asks for sequence diagrams, flowcharts, class diagrams, or architecture visualizations in Mermaid
sf-diagram-mermaid.doNotTriggerWhen: user wants PNG/SVG image output (use sf-diagram-nanobananapro), or non-Salesforce systems

sf-diagram-nanobananapro.triggerWhen: user asks for PNG/SVG output, UI mockups, wireframes, visual ERDs, or says 'generate image' / 'create mockup'
sf-diagram-nanobananapro.doNotTriggerWhen: text-based Mermaid diagrams (use sf-diagram-mermaid), or non-visual documentation tasks

sf-ai-agentforce-testing.triggerWhen: user tests Agentforce agents, runs sf agent test commands, creates test specs, validates topic routing, or analyzes agent test coverage
sf-ai-agentforce-testing.doNotTriggerWhen: Apex unit tests (use sf-testing), building agents (use sf-ai-agentforce), or Agent Script DSL (use sf-ai-agentscript)

sf-ai-agentscript.description: Agent Script DSL for deterministic Agentforce agents
sf-ai-agentscript.triggerWhen: user writes or edits .agent files, builds FSM-based agents, uses Agent Script CLI (sf agent generate/publish), or asks about deterministic agent patterns
sf-ai-agentscript.doNotTriggerWhen: Setup UI agent building (use sf-ai-agentforce), agent testing (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), or persona design (use sf-ai-agentforce-persona)

sf-permissions.description: Permission Set analysis and hierarchy visualization
sf-permissions.triggerWhen: user asks 'who has access to X?', analyzes permission sets/groups, or touches .permissionset-meta.xml / .permissionsetgroup-meta.xml files
sf-permissions.doNotTriggerWhen: creating new metadata (use sf-metadata), deploying permission sets (use sf-deploy), or Apex sharing logic (use sf-apex)

sf-ai-agentforce-observability.description: Extract and analyze Agentforce session tracing data from Data Cloud
sf-ai-agentforce-observability.triggerWhen: user extracts STDM data from Data Cloud, analyzes agent session traces, debugs agent conversations via telemetry, or works with .parquet files from Agentforce
sf-ai-agentforce-observability.doNotTriggerWhen: testing agents (use sf-ai-agentforce-testing), Apex debug logs (use sf-debug), or building agents (use sf-ai-agentforce)

sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.description: Cross-cutting OmniStudio analysis for namespace detection, dependency mapping, and impact analysis
sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.triggerWhen: user asks about OmniStudio dependencies, wants namespace detection (Core vs vlocity_cmt vs vlocity_ins), needs impact analysis across OmniStudio components, or requests dependency diagrams
sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyz.doNotTriggerWhen: authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), building FlexCards (use sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard), creating Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or configuring Data Mappers (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper)

sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.description: OmniStudio Data Mapper (formerly DataRaptor) creation and validation with 100-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.triggerWhen: user creates Data Mappers, configures field mappings, works with OmniDataTransform metadata, or asks about DataRaptor/Data Mapper patterns
sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper.doNotTriggerWhen: building Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), or analyzing cross-component dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.description: OmniStudio Integration Procedure creation and validation with 110-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.triggerWhen: user creates Integration Procedures, adds DataRaptor steps, configures Remote Actions, or reviews existing IP configurations
sf-industry-commoncore-integration-proce.doNotTriggerWhen: building OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), creating Data Mappers directly (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper), or analyzing cross-component dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.description: Salesforce Industries Common Core callable Apex generation and review for System.Callable and legacy Open Interface migrations
sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.triggerWhen: user creates or reviews System.Callable classes, migrates VlocityOpenInterface / VlocityOpenInterface2, or builds Industries callable extensions used by OmniStudio, Integration Procedures, or DataRaptors
sf-industry-commoncore-callable-apex.doNotTriggerWhen: generic Apex classes/triggers (use sf-apex), building Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), authoring OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), configuring Data Mappers (use sf-industry-commoncore-datamapper), or analyzing namespace/dependency issues (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.description: OmniStudio OmniScript creation and validation with 120-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.triggerWhen: user creates OmniScripts, designs step flows, configures element types, or reviews existing OmniScript configurations
sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript.doNotTriggerWhen: building FlexCards (use sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard), creating Integration Procedures directly (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or analyzing dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)

sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.description: OmniStudio FlexCard creation and validation with 130-point scoring
sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.triggerWhen: user creates FlexCards, configures data sources, designs card layouts, or asks about OmniUiCard metadata
sf-industry-commoncore-flexcard.doNotTriggerWhen: building OmniScripts (use sf-industry-commoncore-omniscript), creating Integration Procedures (use sf-industry-commoncore-integration-procedure), or analyzing dependencies (use sf-industry-commoncore-omnistudio-analyze)
